title: Experimental observation of entropic-singularity-induced nonadditive quantum communication in a qutrit platypus channel
authors: Yu Guo, Bo-Xuan Wang, Xiao-Min Hu, Yun-Feng Huang, Chuan-Feng Li, Guang-Can Guo, Bi-Heng Liu
abstract: The nonadditivity of channel capacity is a defining feature that distinguishes quantum communication from classical communication. In the quantum realm, the channel capacity is determined by coherent information, which is defined through the von Neumann entropies of the output and its environment. Despite its fundamental importance, experimental evidence of such nonadditive quantum communication has been elusive because of the complexity of the required quantum channel. Here, we experimentally observe entropic-singularity-induced coherent-information nonadditivity using the qutrit platypus channel implemented on a photonic platform. By preparing six-dimensional photonic entanglement, we directly measure the coherent information of a platypus channel, a qubit amplitude damping channel, and their joint uses, revealing a clear violation of additivity. Quantum process tomography further reveals the entropic singularity responsible for this effect, demonstrating how singular entropy landscapes in low-dimensional channels can enhance quantum communication beyond additive limits.
summary: This paper reports the first experimental observation of nonadditive quantum communication induced by entropic singularities. By using a photonic setup to combine a qutrit platypus channel and an amplitude damping channel, the authors showed that the joint capacity is greater than the sum of individual capacities. This demonstrates that specific types of noise can be exploited to enhance information transmission.
main_problem: The paper investigates the experimental observation of nonadditivity in quantum channel capacity, specifically whether entropic singularities can drive superadditive quantum communication.
main_result: The researchers experimentally demonstrated a violation of additivity by more than 20 standard deviations, showing that the joint coherent information of a qutrit platypus channel and a qubit amplitude damping channel exceeds the sum of their individual capacities.
method: The study uses a photonic platform to implement the channels, employing quantum state and process tomography on 6-dimensional entangled photon states to measure von Neumann entropies and coherent information.
model_or_system: A photonic implementation using a 6-dimensional entanglement source generated via Spontaneous Parametric Down-Conversion (SPDC) in a BBO crystal, implementing a qutrit platypus channel (M3) and a qubit amplitude damping channel (A1/2).
key_observables: Coherent information (Q1), von Neumann entropies of the channel output and environment, and the nonadditivity parameter (Delta).
important_parameters: Damping parameter gamma = 1/2 for the amplitude damping channel; dimension d=3 for the platypus channel; process fidelities exceeding 0.98.
main_assumptions: The experiment assumes that the reconstructed density matrices from tomography accurately represent the true quantum states and that the purification method correctly captures the environment's entropy.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the schematic of the 6D entanglement source, the implementation of the combined M3 and A1/2 channels using optical components, the setup for quantum state tomography, and the encoding strategy for spatial modes.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of nonadditivity, describes the experimental photonic setup and channel implementation, details the tomography and measurement methods, presents the results showing the violation of additivity and the role of entropic singularities, and concludes with the implications of the findings.
why_interesting: This is highly relevant to researchers in open quantum systems and quantum optics as it provides experimental evidence for how singular entropy landscapes in noisy channels can be leveraged to enhance quantum communication rates beyond additive limits.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: Experimental setup. (a) Preparation of the 6-dimensional entangled state. A 404 nm pump laser is expanded into a 2×3 parallel beam array, which coherently pumps a BBO crystal to generate entangled photon pairs via SPDC. The two photons are separated by a PBS, after which one photon undergoes a local unitary operation to achieve the target input states for the channels. (b) Realization of the channel M3 ⊗A1/2 using PBSAs, HWPAs, and a PBS. (d) Quantum state tomography of the 6-dimensional quantum state. (e) Encoding strategy of the six-dimensional path modes. PBS, polarizing beam splitter; HWP, half-wave plate; IF, interference filter; RM, reflection mirror; SPD, single-photon...

FIG. 3: Experimental observation of coherent information nonadditivity for the joint channel M3 ⊗A1/2. For the input state ρ(r1, r2, r3), we vary one parameter while fixing the other two at their respective optimal values and experimentally measure the corresponding value of ∆(r1, r2, r3) (blue bars). The error bars are obtained via Monte Carlo simulations.
